bibliographic_data.title: Observation of Dipolar Spin-ice--like Correlations in the Quantum Spin Ice Candidate Ce$_2$Sn$_2$O$_7$
bibliographic_data.abstract: The Ce2X2O7 (X=Sn, Hf, Zr) family of cubic pyrochlores has emerged as one of the most promising classes of Quantum Spin Ice candidates. However, understanding their microscopic exchange Hamiltonian and spin correlations has been hampered by varying sample quality, and poor signal-to-noise in the existing neutron data due to a small Ce3+ magnetic dipole moment. In this work, we overcome these challenges and report single-crystal diffuse neutron scattering from hydrothermally grown Ce2Sn2O7 – the highest quality crystals obtained to date for the Ce2X2O7 family. In contrast to the broad diffuse scattering observed in Ce2Hf2O7 and Ce2Zr2O7, we find highly structured diffuse scattering from Ce2Sn2O7 featuring strong intensities along the Brillouin zone boundaries. The observed Q-dependence disagrees with predictions of the nearest neighbour XYZ model commonly used for Ce2X2O7, but is remarkably similar to the diffuse scattering observed in classical Dipolar Spin Ice. Our study highlights the importance of further neighbour interactions in determining the low energy physics of the Ce-pyrochlores, and calls for a revision of the current theoretical framework to incorporate their effects.

core_content.novelty_claim: The study reports single-crystal diffuse neutron scattering from hydrothermally grown Ce2Sn2O7, revealing highly structured diffuse scattering along the Brillouin zone boundaries that disagrees with predictions of the nearest neighbour XYZ model and is similar to classical Dipolar Spin Ice.
core_content.key_methodology: Single-crystal diffuse neutron scattering was performed on high-quality Ce2Sn2O7 crystals grown via hydrothermal methods. The data were compared against theoretical models, particularly the nearest neighbour XYZ model commonly used for Ce2X2O7.
outcomes: Highly structured diffuse scattering along Brillouin zone boundaries observed in Ce2Sn2O7
Q-dependence of scattering disagrees with predictions from the nearest neighbour XYZ model
Similarities found between Ce2Sn2O7 and classical Dipolar Spin Ice
core_content.full_text_markdown: The study focuses on the Ce2X2O7 family, which has emerged as promising candidates for Quantum Spin Ice. However, understanding their microscopic exchange Hamiltonian and spin correlations is challenging due to sample quality issues and poor signal-to-noise in existing neutron data. The authors report single-crystal diffuse neutron scattering from hydrothermally grown Ce2Sn2O7, the highest quality crystals obtained so far. Unlike previous observations of broad diffuse scattering in Ce2Hf2O7 and Ce2Zr2O7, Ce2Sn2O7 shows highly structured diffuse scattering along Brillouin zone boundaries. This Q-dependence contradicts predictions from the nearest neighbour XYZ model but is similar to classical Dipolar Spin Ice. The study emphasizes the importance of further neighbor interactions in determining the low energy physics of these materials and calls for a revision of current theoretical frameworks.
